revenue: $44.38 billion, down 10.5% YoY compared to $49.57 billion in Q2 2024
net_income: $2.49 billion, down 43.8% YoY compared to $4.43 billion in Q2 2024
key_metrics: Diluted EPS of $1.45, down from $2.43 YoY
Total Revenues and Other Income of $44.82 billion, down 12.4% YoY
Cash and cash equivalents decreased to $4.06 billion from $6.78 billion at year-end 2024
qoq_changes: Estimated sequential revenue decline from approximately $46.1 billion in Q1 2025 to $44.38 billion in Q2 2025
Estimated sequential net income decline from approximately $3.50 billion in Q1 2025 to $2.49 billion in Q2 2025
new_risks: Integration risks and realization of anticipated synergies associated with the Hess Corporation transaction
Geopolitical volatility including the conflict in the Middle East and Russia/Ukraine affecting supply chains
Uncertainties regarding the pace and scale of development for large carbon capture and offset markets
management_tone: Cautious, given the significant year-over-year declines in both revenue and net income, compounded by macroeconomic and geopolitical uncertainties.
analyst_note: Chevron's Q2 2025 results highlight a challenging quarter with net income falling nearly 44% year-over-year, driven by lower revenues and sustained operating costs. Focus remains on the successful integration of Hess Corporation and navigating a volatile global energy landscape.
